title: TC-DFU-001: Voyager Free 60+ Device Artwork Rendering on Home Screen (iOS)
steps: Precondition: Poly Voyager Free 60+ is paired and connected to iOS device.
Launch Poly Studio Mobile app.
Observe the Home screen device artwork area.
Navigate to: Device → Device Info → verify model name displayed.
Return to Home screen.
Force-kill the app (swipe up from app switcher).
Reopen app and observe Home screen device artwork again.
expected: Device artwork (product image) for Poly Voyager Free 60+ is displayed on the Home screen. Model name reads 'Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC' or 'Poly Voyager Free 60+'. Artwork persists after app restart. Actual (If Bug): Artwork area shows a generic placeholder icon or blank space, indicating the app failed to load the device-specific asset.

title: TC-DFU-002: Firmware Update from v231.591.0.5046 (MR1.5) to Latest — Success Path (iOS)
steps: Precondition: Poly Voyager Free 60+ connected to iOS device. Current firmware version confirmed as v231.591.0.5046 (MR1.5) via Device → Device Info.
Ensure battery > 50% on both earbuds and charge case.
Ensure no active call or media streaming.
Navigate to: Device → Firmware Update.
Tap 'Check for Update'.
If update is available, tap 'Download & Install'.
Observe progress bar during download phase.
Observe progress bar during installation phase.
Wait for completion message.
Navigate to: Device → Device Info and verify new firmware version.
expected: App detects latest firmware. Download completes to 100%. Installation completes to 100%. Device reboots successfully. Device Info shows updated firmware version > v231.591.0.5046. Actual (If Bug): Update fails at any stage (download stalls, installation fails at X%, device enters recovery mode, or app shows generic 'Update Failed' without actionable error message.)

title: TC-DFU-003: Firmware Update Failure from v231.591.0.5046 (MR1.5) — Error Handling & Rollback Verification (iOS)
steps: Precondition: Poly Voyager Free 60+ on v231.591.0.5046 (MR1.5) connected to iOS.
Navigate to: Device → Firmware Update → Check for Update → Start Download & Install.
During installation phase, simulate a failure condition: place the earbuds back in the charge case and close the lid mid-installation.
Wait 60 seconds.
Open charge case lid and observe app state.
Check the error message displayed.
Tap any available retry/dismiss button.
Navigate to: Device → Device Info and verify firmware version.
Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Transparency Mode → verify Environment (Surround) and Speech (Free) sliders are functional and persist.
expected: App detects installation interruption and displays a clear error message (e.g., 'Update interrupted. Please try again.' or 'Installation failed. Device is in recovery mode.'). Device does not enter an unrecoverable bricked state. Firmware version either remains at v231.591.0.5046 (rollback) or shows a partial version if recovery mode is active. Audio settings remain accessible and functional. Actual (If Bug): App shows generic 'Something went wrong' with no retry path, device becomes unresponsive, or settings are wiped/reset after failure.

title: TC-DFU-004: Combined Failure — Missing Device Artwork Precedes DFU Failure (iOS)
steps: Precondition: Poly Voyager Free 60+ connected to iOS device.
Launch app and observe Home screen device artwork.
If artwork is missing (placeholder/blank), document this as a pre-condition failure indicator.
Without fixing the artwork issue, navigate to: Device → Firmware Update.
Tap 'Check for Update' and proceed with available update.
Observe the entire DFU flow.
If DFU fails, note the error message and device state.
Force-kill app, reopen, and check if artwork now renders.
Navigate to: Device → Device Info and verify model identification string.
expected: If artwork is missing on Home screen, the DFU flow is expected to either: (a) fail with a specific error indicating device identification failure, or (b) succeed but with degraded UX. If DFU fails, the error message should be specific (e.g., 'Unable to identify device model. Update cannot proceed.') rather than generic. After app restart, artwork may render correctly if the issue was a transient loading failure. Actual (If Bug): Missing artwork + DFU fails with generic error, indicating a deeper device communication stack issue where the app cannot properly identify the Voyager Free 60+ model, affecting both UI rendering and firmware update capability.

title: TC-DFU-005: Device Artwork as Health Indicator — Immediate State Trigger on App Launch (iOS)
steps: Precondition: Poly Voyager Free 60+ connected to iOS device. App is fully closed (not backgrounded).
Enable Airplane Mode on iOS device.
Launch Poly Studio Mobile app.
Observe Home screen device artwork area.
Disable Airplane Mode and wait for Bluetooth reconnection.
Observe artwork area again after reconnection.
Navigate to: Device → Firmware Update and verify update availability check behavior.
Force-kill app, reopen with Bluetooth already connected, observe artwork.
expected: With Airplane Mode ON (no Bluetooth): Home screen shows 'No device connected' or similar state; artwork area is empty/placeholder. After Bluetooth reconnects: artwork loads within 5 seconds, confirming the device communication stack is healthy. Firmware update check returns available updates. After app restart with connected device: artwork renders immediately on launch. Actual (If Bug): Artwork remains blank/placeholder even after Bluetooth reconnects, indicating the app's device identification pipeline failed to resolve the model — this is a health indicator that DFU will likely also fail.

title: TC-DFU-006: Firmware Update from v231.591.0.5046 (MR1.5) — Deep Lifecycle Resilience (iOS)
steps: Precondition: Poly Voyager Free 60+ on v231.591.0.5046 (MR1.5) connected to iOS.
Navigate to: Device → Firmware Update → Start Download.
During download phase (at ~30%), press Home button to background the app.
Wait 30 seconds.
Reopen app from app switcher.
Observe download progress state.
During installation phase (at ~50%), press Home button to background the app.
Wait 30 seconds.
Reopen app from app switcher.
Observe installation progress state.
Wait for completion.
Verify final firmware version in Device Info.
Verify audio settings persistence: Navigate to Transparency Mode → set Environment Surround to 75 and Speech Free to 40 → switch between tabs → confirm values persist.
expected: Download and installation progress persists correctly after app backgrounding and foregrounding. Progress bar resumes from where it left off (not reset to 0). Installation completes successfully. Final firmware version is updated. Transparency Mode sub-setting values (Surround=75, Speech=40) persist after switching between Environment and Speech tabs. Actual (If Bug): Progress resets to 0 after foregrounding, installation fails silently, or sub-setting values are lost after the DFU lifecycle.

title: TC-DFU-007: Firmware Update Failure Recovery — Retry Mechanism Validation (iOS)
steps: Precondition: Poly Voyager Free 60+ on v231.591.0.5046 (MR1.5) connected to iOS.
Navigate to: Device → Firmware Update → Start Download & Install.
Force a failure by disconnecting Bluetooth from iOS Control Center during installation phase.
Wait for error state to appear.
Observe error message content and available actions.
Tap 'Retry' or equivalent button.
Observe if DFU restarts from beginning or resumes.
Reconnect Bluetooth when prompted.
Complete the update successfully.
Verify device artwork on Home screen post-update.
Verify Transparency Mode sub-setting values persist post-update.
expected: Error message clearly states 'Bluetooth disconnected. Update interrupted.' Retry button is available. Tapping Retry restarts the DFU process from the download phase (not installation). After reconnecting Bluetooth, the update completes successfully. Device artwork renders correctly post-update. Transparency Mode sub-settings retain default or previously set values. Actual (If Bug): No retry button, app shows unrecoverable error, device enters recovery mode with no exit path, or artwork is missing after recovery.

title: TC-DFU-008: Device Artwork & DFU Combined — iOS-Specific Memory Pressure Test
steps: Precondition: Poly Voyager Free 60+ connected to iOS device on v231.591.0.5046 (MR1.5).
Launch Poly Studio Mobile app and verify artwork renders on Home screen.
Open Safari and load 10+ heavy media tabs to induce iOS memory pressure.
Switch back to Poly Studio Mobile app.
Observe if artwork is still rendered or has been replaced by placeholder.
Without closing Safari tabs, navigate to: Device → Firmware Update → Start DFU.
Observe DFU behavior under memory pressure.
Complete or fail the update.
Force-kill all apps, reopen Poly Studio Mobile, verify artwork and device state.
expected: Under iOS memory pressure, the app may reload. Upon returning to the app, artwork should re-render within 5 seconds (not remain blank). DFU should either complete successfully or show a clear error if memory was insufficient. After full app restart, artwork renders correctly and device is in a known good state. Actual (If Bug): Artwork fails to re-render after memory pressure (stays blank), DFU fails silently or crashes the app, or device enters an unknown state requiring manual repair.
